memories: User has a recurring subscription to a cheese-of-the-month club despite rarely hosting guests.
User adopted a senior black cat from the shelter that prefers sleeping inside the taxidermy display case.
User identifies strongly with the goth subculture, finding beauty and solace in dark aesthetics, melancholic music, and Victorian-era literature.
User's kitchen pantry is organized by expiration date and contains no fewer than seven varieties of loose-leaf black tea collected from specialty shops.

User begins each Sunday morning by brewing black coffee in a vintage French press, then sitting by the bay window to watch crows gather in the oak tree behind User's apartment building, finding the ritual more meditative than any music.
User's favorite possessions are their collection of antique taxidermy insects and their leather-bound journal.

User inherited a tarnished silver pocket watch that belonged to User's great-grandfather, keeping it on the desk as a paperweight and occasionally winding it during late-night debugging sessions, though the watch hasn't kept time since 1987.
User maintains a small aquarium of black neon tetras on a shelf above the workstation, finding the synchronized swimming hypnotic during stressful code deployments.
User works as a database administrator.
User finds that listening to dark ambient music and reading Edgar Allan Poe is the most effective way for them to feel calm and centered during stressful times.
User's favorite comfort food is miso soup with extra tofu, which User makes from scratch.
query: My best friend just had surgery and is feeling really low. I want to give her a gift that feels deeply personal and comforting to help her through this. What are some ideas that go beyond the usual flowers and chocolate?
